description: Fast JavaScript runtime, package manager, bundler, and test runner.
option_watch: Automatically restart the process on file change
option_hot: Enable auto reload in the Bun runtime, test runner, or bundler
option_smol: Use less memory, but run garbage collection more often
option_no_clear_screen: Disable clearing the terminal screen on reload when --hot or --watch is enabled
option_preload: Import a module before other modules are loaded
option_inspect_wait: Activate Bun's debugger, wait for a connection before executing
option_inspect_brk: Activate Bun's debugger, set breakpoint on first line of code and wait
option_cpu_prof: Start CPU profiler and write profile to disk on exit
option_heap_prof: Generate V8 heap snapshot on exit (.heapsnapshot)
option_if_present: Exit without an error if the entrypoint does not exist
option_no_install: Disable auto install in the Bun runtime
option_install: Configure auto-install behavior. One of "auto" (default, auto-installs when no node_modules), "fallback" (missing packages only), "force" (always).
option_i: Auto-install dependencies during execution. Equivalent to --install=fallback.
option_print: Evaluate argument as a script and print the result
option_prefer_offline: Skip staleness checks for packages in the Bun runtime and resolve from disk
option_prefer_latest: Use the latest matching versions of packages in the Bun runtime, always checking npm
option_port: Set the default port for Bun.serve
option_fetch_preconnect: Preconnect to a URL while code is loading
option_max_http_header_size: Set the maximum size of HTTP headers in bytes. Default is 16KiB
option_dns_result_order: Set the default order of DNS lookup results. Valid orders: verbatim (default), ipv4first, ipv6first
option_expose_gc: Expose gc() on the global object. Has no effect on Bun.gc().
option_no_deprecation: Suppress all reporting of the custom deprecation.
option_throw_deprecation: Determine whether or not deprecation warnings result in errors.
option_zero_fill_buffers: Boolean to force Buffer.allocUnsafe(size) to be zero-filled.
option_use_system_ca: Use the system's trusted certificate authorities
option_no_addons: Throw an error if process.dlopen is called, and disable export condition "node-addons"
option_console_depth: Set the default depth for console.log object inspection (default: 2)
option_user_agent: Set the default User-Agent header for HTTP requests
option_cron_period: Cron period for cron execution mode
option_elide_lines: Number of lines of script output shown when using --filter (default: 10). Set to 0 to show all lines.
option_revision: Print version with revision and exit
option_filter: Run a script in all workspace packages matching the pattern
option_bun: Force a script or package to use Bun's runtime instead of Node.js (via symlinking node)
option_shell: Control the shell used for package.json scripts. Supports either 'bun' or 'system'
option_workspaces: Run a script in all workspace packages (from the "workspaces" field in package.json)
option_parallel: Run multiple scripts concurrently with Foreman-style output
option_sequential: Run multiple scripts sequentially with Foreman-style output
option_no_exit_on_error: Continue running other scripts when one fails (with --parallel/--sequential)
option_env_file: Load environment variables from the specified file(s)
option_cwd: Absolute path to resolve files & entry points from. This just changes the process' cwd.
option_config: Specify path to Bun config file. Default $cwd/bunfig.toml
cmd_run_desc: Execute a file with Bun or run a package.json script
cmd_x_desc: Execute a package binary (CLI), installing if needed (bunx)
cmd_repl_desc: Start a REPL session with Bun
cmd_exec_desc: Run a shell script directly with Bun
cmd_install_desc: Install dependencies for a package.json (bun i)
cmd_add_desc: Add a dependency to package.json (bun a)
cmd_remove_desc: Remove a dependency from package.json (bun rm)
cmd_outdated_desc: Display latest versions of outdated dependencies
cmd_link_desc: Register or link a local npm package
cmd_publish_desc: Publish a package to the npm registry
cmd_info_desc: Display package metadata from the registry
cmd_why_desc: Explain why a package is installed
cmd_build_desc: Bundle TypeScript & JavaScript into a single file
cmd_init_desc: Start an empty Bun project from a built-in template
cmd_create_desc: Create a new project from a template (bun c)
cmd_upgrade_desc: Upgrade to latest version of Bun
cmd_feedback_desc: Provide feedback to the Bun team
test_option_timeout: Set the per-test timeout in milliseconds, default is 5000.
test_option_rerun_each: Re-run each test file <NUMBER> times, helps catch certain bugs
test_option_retry: Default retry count for all tests, overridden by per-test { retry: N }
test_option_todo: Include tests that are marked with "test.todo()"
test_option_only: Run only tests that are marked with "test.only()" or "describe.only()"
test_option_pass_with_no_tests: Exit with code 0 when no tests are found
test_option_seed: Set the random seed for test randomization
test_option_coverage_reporter: Report coverage in 'text' and/or 'lcov'. Defaults to 'text'.
test_option_coverage_dir: Directory for coverage files. Defaults to 'coverage'.
test_option_bail: Exit the test suite after <NUMBER> failures. If you do not specify a number, it defaults to 1.
test_option_test_name_pattern: Run only tests with a name that matches the given regex.
test_option_reporter: Test output reporter format. Available: 'junit' (requires --reporter-outfile), 'dots'. Default: console output.
test_option_reporter_outfile: Output file path for the reporter format (required with --reporter).
test_option_only_failures: Only display test failures, hiding passing tests.
test_option_max_concurrency: Maximum number of concurrent tests to execute at once. Default is 20.
test_option_path_ignore_patterns: Glob patterns for test file paths to ignore.
test_option_changed: Only run test files affected by changed files according to git. Optionally pass a commit or branch to compare against.
test_option_isolate: Run each test file in a fresh global object. Leaked handles from one file cannot affect another.
test_option_parallel: Run test files in parallel using N worker processes. Implies --isolate. Defaults to CPU core count.
test_option_parallel_delay: Milliseconds the first --parallel worker must be busy before spawning the rest. 0 spawns all immediately. Default 5.
test_option_shard: Run a subset of test files, e.g. '--shard=1/3' runs the first of three shards. Useful for splitting tests across multiple CI jobs.
x_option_package: Specify package to install when binary name differs from package name
install_option_no_save: Don't update package.json or save a lockfile
install_option_dry_run: Perform a dry run without making changes
install_option_force: Always request the latest versions from the registry & reinstall all dependencies
install_option_cache_dir: Store & load cached data from a specific directory path
install_option_no_verify: Skip verifying integrity of newly downloaded packages
install_option_ignore_scripts: Skip lifecycle scripts in the project's package.json (dependency scripts are never run)
install_option_trust: Add to trustedDependencies in the project's package.json and install the package(s)
install_option_backend: Platform-specific optimizations for installing dependencies. Possible values: "hardlink" (default), "symlink", "copyfile"
install_option_registry: Use a specific registry by default, overriding .npmrc, bunfig.toml and environment variables
install_option_concurrent_scripts: Maximum number of concurrent jobs for lifecycle scripts (default: 2x CPU cores)
install_option_network_concurrency: Maximum number of concurrent network requests (default 48)
install_option_lockfile_only: Generate a lockfile without installing dependencies
install_option_minimum_release_age: Only install packages published at least N seconds ago (security feature)
install_option_cpu: Override CPU architecture for optional dependencies (e.g., x64, arm64, * for all)
install_option_os: Override operating system for optional dependencies (e.g., linux, darwin, * for all)
install_option_exact: Add the exact version instead of the ^range
install_option_analyze: Analyze & install all dependencies of files passed as arguments recursively (using Bun's bundler)
install_option_only_missing: Only add dependencies to package.json if they are not already present
update_option_latest: Update packages to their latest versions
update_option_interactive: Show an interactive list of outdated packages to select for update
audit_option_audit_level: Only print advisories with severity greater than or equal to <level> (low, moderate, high, critical)
audit_option_ignore: Ignore specific CVE IDs from audit
outdated_option_recursive: Check outdated packages in all workspaces
build_option_compile: Generate a standalone Bun executable containing your bundled code. Implies --production
build_option_target: The intended execution environment for the bundle. "browser", "bun" or "node"
build_option_metafile: Write a JSON file with metadata about the build
build_option_format: Specifies the module format to build to. "esm", "cjs" and "iife" are supported. Defaults to "esm", or "cjs" with --bytecode.
build_option_external: Exclude module from transpilation (can use * wildcards). ex: -e react
patch_option_commit: Install a package containing modifications in `dir`
patch_option_patches_dir: The directory to put the patch file in (only if --commit is used)
pm_scan_desc: scan all packages in lockfile for security vulnerabilities
pm_pack_desc: create a tarball of the current workspace
pm_bin_desc: print the path to bin folder
pm_list_desc: list the dependency tree according to the current lockfile
pm_why_desc: show dependency tree explaining why a package is installed
pm_view_desc: view package metadata from the registry (use `bun info` instead)
pm_version_desc: bump the version in package.json and create a git tag
pm_hash_desc: generate & print the hash of the current lockfile
pm_hash_string_desc: print the string used to hash the lockfile
pm_hash_print_desc: print the hash stored in the current lockfile
pm_cache_desc: print the path to the cache folder
pm_migrate_desc: migrate another package manager's lockfile without installing anything
pm_untrusted_desc: print current untrusted dependencies with scripts
pm_trust_desc: run scripts for untrusted dependencies and add to `trustedDependencies`
pm_default_trusted_desc: print the default trusted dependencies list
install_option_filter: Install packages for the matching workspaces
option_cpu_prof_name: Specify the name of the CPU profile file
option_cpu_prof_dir: Specify the directory where the CPU profile will be saved
option_cpu_prof_md: Output CPU profile in markdown format (grep-friendly, designed for LLM analysis)
option_cpu_prof_interval: Specify the sampling interval in microseconds for CPU profiling (default: 1000)
option_heap_prof_name: Specify the name of the heap profile file
option_heap_prof_dir: Specify the directory where the heap profile will be saved
option_heap_prof_md: Generate markdown heap profile on exit (for CLI analysis)
install_option_ca: Provide a Certificate Authority signing certificate
install_option_cafile: The same as --ca, but is a file path to the certificate
install_option_quiet: Only show tarball name when packing
run_option_main_fields: Main fields to lookup in package.json. Defaults to --target dependent
run_option_preserve_symlinks_main: Preserve symlinks when resolving the main entry point
run_option_define: Substitute K:V while parsing, e.g. --define process.env.NODE_ENV:"development". Values are parsed as JSON.
run_option_drop: Remove function calls, e.g. --drop=console removes all console.* calls.
run_option_feature: Enable a feature flag for dead-code elimination, e.g. --feature=SUPER_SECRET
run_option_loader: Parse files with .ext:loader, e.g. --loader .js:jsx. Valid loaders: js, jsx, ts, tsx, json, toml, text, file, wasm, napi
run_option_no_macros: Disable macros from being executed in the bundler, transpiler and runtime
run_option_jsx_factory: Changes the function called when compiling JSX elements using the classic JSX runtime
run_option_jsx_fragment: Changes the function called when compiling JSX fragments
run_option_jsx_import_source: Declares the module specifier to be used for importing the jsx and jsxs factory functions. Default: "react"
run_option_jsx_side_effects: Treat JSX elements as having side effects (disable pure annotations)
test_option_test_worker: (internal) Run as a --parallel worker, receiving files over IPC.
build_option_compile_exec_argv: Prepend arguments to the standalone executable's execArgv
build_option_compile_autoload_dotenv: Enable autoloading of .env files in standalone executable (default: true)
build_option_no_compile_autoload_dotenv: Disable autoloading of .env files in standalone executable
build_option_compile_autoload_bunfig: Enable autoloading of bunfig.toml in standalone executable (default: true)
build_option_no_compile_autoload_bunfig: Disable autoloading of bunfig.toml in standalone executable
build_option_compile_autoload_tsconfig: Enable autoloading of tsconfig.json at runtime in standalone executable (default: false)
build_option_no_compile_autoload_tsconfi: Disable autoloading of tsconfig.json at runtime in standalone executable
build_option_compile_autoload_package_js: Enable autoloading of package.json at runtime in standalone executable (default: false)
build_option_no_compile_autoload_package: Disable autoloading of package.json at runtime in standalone executable
build_option_compile_executable_path: Path to a Bun executable to use for cross-compilation instead of downloading
build_option_metafile_md: Write a markdown file with a visualization of the module graph (LLM-friendly)
build_option_banner: Add a banner to the bundled output such as "use client"; for a bundle being used with RSCs
build_option_footer: Add a footer to the bundled output such as // built with bun!
build_option_root: Root directory used for multiple entry points
build_option_public_path: A prefix to be appended to any import paths in bundled code
build_option_allow_unresolved: Allow unresolved dynamic import()/require() specifiers matching these glob patterns. Use '<empty>' for opaque specifiers. Default is '*' (allow all).
build_option_reject_unresolved: Fail the build on any dynamic import()/require() specifier that cannot be resolved at build time.
build_option_packages: Add dependencies to bundle or keep them external. "external", "bundle" is supported. Defaults to "bundle".
build_option_entry_naming: Customize entry point filenames. Defaults to "[dir]/[name].[ext]"
build_option_react_fast_refresh: Enable React Fast Refresh transform (does not emit hot-module code, use this for testing)
build_option_no_bundle: Transpile file only, do not bundle
build_option_emit_dce_annotations: Re-emit DCE annotations in bundles. Enabled by default unless --minify-whitespace is passed.
build_option_keep_names: Preserve original function and class names when minifying
build_option_css_chunking: Chunk CSS files together to reduce duplicated CSS loaded in a browser. Only has an effect when multiple entrypoints import CSS
build_option_app: (EXPERIMENTAL) Build a web app for production using Bun Bake.
build_option_env: Inline environment variables into the bundle as process.env.${name}. Defaults to 'disable'. To inline environment variables matching a prefix, use my prefix like 'FOO_PUBLIC_*'.
build_option_windows_hide_console: When using --compile targeting Windows, prevent a Command prompt from opening alongside the executable
build_option_windows_icon: When using --compile targeting Windows, assign an executable icon
build_option_windows_title: When using --compile targeting Windows, set the executable product name
build_option_windows_publisher: When using --compile targeting Windows, set the executable company name
build_option_windows_version: When using --compile targeting Windows, set the executable version (e.g. 1.2.3.4)
build_option_windows_description: When using --compile targeting Windows, set the executable description
build_option_windows_copyright: When using --compile targeting Windows, set the executable copyright
publish_option_access: Set access level for scoped packages
publish_option_otp: Provide a one-time password for authentication
publish_option_auth_type: Specify the type of one-time password authentication (default is 'web')
publish_option_gzip_level: Specify a custom compression level for gzip. Default is 9.
publish_option_tolerate_republish: Don't exit with code 1 when republishing over an existing version number
upgrade_option_canary: Install the most recent canary version of Bun
feedback_option_email: Set the email address used for this submission
why_option_top: Show only the top dependency tree instead of nested ones
why_option_depth: Maximum depth of the dependency tree to display
init_option_react_tailwind: Initialize a React project with TailwindCSS
init_option_react_shadcn: Initialize a React project with @shadcn/ui and TailwindCSS
pm_pack_option_destination: the directory the tarball will be saved in
pm_pack_option_ignore_scripts: don't run pre/postpack and prepare scripts
pm_pack_option_gzip_level: specify a custom compression level for gzip (0-9, default is 9)
pm_bin_option_g: print the global path to bin folder
pm_list_option_all: list the entire dependency tree according to the current lockfile
pm_version_increment: version increment: patch, minor, major, prepatch, preminor, premajor, prerelease, from-git, or a specific version
